code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test24-JUN-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, potassium, and chloride, with low CO₂, suggesting possible renal impairment and mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating significant renal dysfunction, possibly diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
